engineGuide.h2: Peugeot 4008 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot 4008 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The DV6C 1.6 HDi powered most early 4008 models, offering frugal economy but prone to EGR and DPF issues in urban use.
compatibleVariants: Active 1.6 HDi, Allure 1.6 HDi, Access 1.6 HDi, Style 1.6 HDi
commonFailures: EGR cooler clogging and failure at 70k-100k miles
DPF saturation from short urban journeys
Injector seal leaks causing rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6C Peugeot 4008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Updated DV6 variant with revised EGR mapping, fitted to AWD 4008 models with Mitsubishi-derived transfer case compatibility.
commonFailures: DPF clogging amplified by AWD weight and urban use
EGR valve sticking causing loss of power
Turbo oil seal wear at higher mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6DTED Peugeot 4008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: BlueHDi evolution with AdBlue SCR system, introduced on facelift 4008 models for improved emissions compliance.
compatibleVariants: Active 1.6 BlueHDi, Allure 1.6 BlueHDi, Style 1.6 BlueHDi
commonFailures: AdBlue injector blockage and NOx sensor faults
EGR cooler leaks causing coolant loss
DPF regeneration failures on short trips

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol

commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure causing rattling at 60k-90k miles
High pressure fuel pump wear leading to lean running

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Upgraded THP variant for facelift 4008 and 200 GT models, featuring revised cooling and turbo components for improved reliability.
commonFailures: HPFP failure under high load conditions
Turbo oil feed coking at high mileage
Carbon buildup on intake valves from direct injection
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6CDB Peugeot 4008 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Mitsubishi-sourced naturally aspirated engine fitted to early 4008 Access models; robust but less common in UK market.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing noise at 80k+ miles
CVT transmission compatibility issues during swap

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 4008 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Peugeot 4008 THP Timing Chain Failure - Why EP6 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 4008 Active, Allure, Style 1.6 THP (2012-2021, EP6CDT & EP6CDB)
problems.rootCause: Factory timing chain tensioner design allows slack to develop, causing chain stretch, rattling noise, and potential valve damage if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents engine damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain kit plus valve train inspection, head gasket if needed
repairOptions.longevity: Extends life but doesn't address bottom-end wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage or damaged engines

problems.recommendation: If your 4008 THP is rattling but still runs, act immediately - a preventative chain kit replacement at a specialist (£800-£1,200) is far cheaper than engine replacement. If the chain has snapped or valves are bent, a rebuilt EP6 with upgraded components is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EP6CDT engine prices for your Peugeot 4008

problems.h4: Peugeot 4008 Turbo Wastegate Failure - THP Power Loss & Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 4008 1.6 THP Active, Allure, 200 GT (2012-2021, EP6CDT & EP6CDB)
problems.rootCause: Turbo wastegate actuator (code 1742.89) sticks or fails, causing over/under-boost, loss of power, and engine management lights.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty actuator, recalibrate boost control
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if turbo core is healthy; quick fix

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, recondition turbo with new wastegate, seals, balance
repairOptions.longevity: Good option if engine otherwise healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded turbo components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if turbo failure caused secondary engine damage

problems.recommendation: For isolated wastegate failure on a well-maintained 4008 THP, actuator replacement (£500-£850) is cost-effective. If the turbo has suffered oil starvation or shaft damage, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo components offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for EP6CDB Peugeot 4008 turbo replacement

problems.h4: Peugeot 4008 EGR Cooler Failure - Diesel Power Loss & Coolant Issues
problems.rootCause: EGR cooler cracks or clogs with carbon, causing coolant loss, overheating, white smoke, and DPF regeneration failures.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean or replace EGR valve, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if cooler is intact; doesn't address root cause

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cooler assembly, flush cooling system, update software
repairOptions.longevity: Effective solution if engine block is undamaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with EGR delete option, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if cooler failure caused head gasket or block damage

problems.recommendation: Early EGR valve cleaning (£350-£600) can extend life on low-mileage 4008 diesels. If the cooler has leaked coolant into cylinders, a rebuilt DV6 with EGR fix is the only reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DV6C Peugeot 4008 diesel engine prices

problems.h4: Peugeot 4008 DPF Clogging - Urban Diesel Regeneration Failure
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 4008 1.6 HDi/BlueHDi all trims (2012-2021, DV6 series)
problems.rootCause: Short journeys prevent active DPF regeneration; soot accumulation causes blockage, limp mode, and eventual engine damage from excessive backpressure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic reset, forced regen cycle, DPF additive top-up
repairOptions.longevity: Only works if blockage is mild; preventative maintenance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove DPF, professional ash/soot cleaning, reinstall and test
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for moderate blockages; doesn't fix underlying usage pattern

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt DV6 with DPF Fix
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with DPF delete option or upgraded filter, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if DPF failure caused turbo or engine damage

problems.recommendation: If your 4008 diesel is used mainly for short trips, invest in regular motorway runs to aid regeneration. For confirmed DPF blockage with error codes, professional cleaning (£450-£750) is viable; if the engine has suffered damage from backpressure, a rebuilt unit is the safer investment.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 4008 DV6FD BlueHDi engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 4008
finalCta.paragraph: The Peugeot 4008's Mitsubishi-derived engines are capable of high mileage when maintained, but known failure points like THP timing chains and HDi EGR coolers can lead to costly repairs. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Peugeot 4008 specialists who understand the platform's unique requirements. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot 4008 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 4008 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 4008 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 4008's 2012-2021 UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Peugeot 4008 launched as a rebadged Mitsubishi ASX, offering three distinct engine families requiring specialist compatibility knowledge.
keyChanges: First UK registrations of Peugeot-badged Mitsubishi GS platform crossover
Introduction of EP6CDT THP petrol and DV6C HDi diesel to 4008 range
Mitsubishi 4B11 2.0L petrol offered on Access trim for robustness
knownFor: Strong initial demand for budget crossover with Peugeot badge
Early EP6CDT timing chain tensioner issues emerging at 60k miles
DV6C EGR cooler failures reported on high-mileage urban examples
4B11 praised for simplicity but rare in UK breaker supply
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2014 Peugeot 4008 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 4008 1.6 THP engine replacement • Peugeot 4008 DV6C diesel price • Mitsubishi 4B11 engine for 4008

years.description: Mid-cycle updates brought minor ECU revisions but the core EP6CDT and DV6C engines remained, with failure patterns becoming well-documented in owner forums.
keyChanges: Minor ECU updates to address early THP turbo wastegate codes
DV6C EGR mapping revised but cooler design unchanged
AWD variants with DV6DTED diesel gained traction in UK fleet market
knownFor: EP6CDT timing chain rattling now a well-known failure pattern
DV6C/DV6DTED DPF clogging accelerated by urban AWD weight
Breaker supply of low-mileage donor engines improving for cost-conscious owners
years.ticker: Peugeot 4008 THP timing chain cost • Peugeot 4008 AWD diesel engine • DV6DTED EGR failure fix

years.preview: Facelift Generation: EP6CDB THP & BlueHDi Diesel
years.description: The 2017 facelift introduced upgraded EP6CDB THP and DV6FD BlueHDi engines with revised components addressing some earlier failure points.
keyChanges: EP6CDB THP 200hp introduced for 200 GT flagship variant
DV6FD BlueHDi replaced DV6C with AdBlue SCR emissions system
Revised turbo wastegate actuators and EGR cooler designs on facelift models
mainEngines: EP6CDT - 1.6L turbo petrol 156hp (continued on lower trims)
DV6FD - 1.6L BlueHDi diesel (all facelift diesel trims)
knownFor: EP6CDB offers improved reliability but HPFP wear still possible at high load
DV6FD BlueHDi AdBlue injector faults emerging as new failure pattern
Facelift models command higher used values, making engine replacement more viable
years.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 4008 facelift 2017-2021 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 4008 200 GT engine price • Peugeot 4008 BlueHDi AdBlue fix • EP6CDB vs EP6CDT compatibility

faq.h2: Peugeot 4008 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Peugeot 4008 engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot 4008 engines typically cost £1,400-£3,900 supply-only depending on engine code and variant, with diesel DV6 units at the lower end and THP petrol units higher due to turbo complexity.
keyPoints: DV6C/BlueHDi diesel rebuilt: £1,400-£3,000 typical range
EP6CDT/EP6CDB THP petrol rebuilt: £1,800-£3,900 typical range
Supply & fit adds £400-£900 depending on specialist location
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Peugeot 4008 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the Peugeot 4008 1.6 THP engine reliable?
items.answer: The EP6CDT/EP6CDB THP engines deliver strong performance but have well-documented timing chain tensioner and turbo wastegate failure patterns requiring proactive maintenance or upgraded rebuilt units.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner failure common at 60k-90k miles
Turbo wastegate actuator (1742.89) sticking causes limp mode
Rebuilt units with upgraded tensioner offer best long-term value
items.cta: Get quotes for upgraded EP6CDT Peugeot 4008 engine replacement

items.question: What causes Peugeot 4008 diesel engines to fail?
items.answer: DV6 series diesel engines in the 4008 commonly fail due to EGR cooler cracks causing coolant loss and DPF clogging from urban short-journey use preventing regeneration.
keyPoints: EGR cooler failure leads to coolant loss and overheating risk
DPF saturation occurs faster on urban AWD variants
BlueHDi DV6FD adds AdBlue injector fault potential
items.cta: Compare rebuilt DV6C Peugeot 4008 diesel engine prices

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Peugeot 4008?
items.answer: Engine swaps in the Peugeot 4008 are complex due to Mitsubishi GS platform wiring and mounting; stick to the original engine code for your variant to ensure compatibility and avoid ECU issues.
keyPoints: EP6CDT and EP6CDB are not directly interchangeable without ECU remap
DV6C, DV6DTED and DV6FD have different EGR/AdBlue systems
Mitsubishi 4B11 requires CVT transmission compatibility check
items.cta: Find a Peugeot 4008 engine specialist for compatibility advice

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Peugeot 4008?
items.answer: With used Peugeot 4008 values typically £5,000-£10,000, a rebuilt engine at £1,400-£3,900 often represents better value than scrapping, especially for well-maintained Active or Allure trims.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost is 30-60% of typical used vehicle value
Upgraded rebuilt units address known failure points for longevity
Supply & fit service preserves vehicle utility without dealer pricing

items.answer: Engines supplied through EngineMarket's UK network include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard, with some specialists offering extended 24-month options on remanufactured units.

items.question: How long does Peugeot 4008 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engine delivery typically takes 1-3 working days from UK stock; full supply & fit service requires 2-4 days including booking, fitting, and post-installation testing.

items.question: How do I check my Peugeot 4008 engine code?
items.answer: Your Peugeot 4008 engine code is stamped on the engine block near the gearbox, listed on the V5C logbook under "engine number", and can be instantly verified by entering your registration on EngineMarket.
keyPoints: V5C logbook shows engine number prefix matching code (e.g., DV6C)
Physical stamp location: engine block side, near transmission bellhousing
Registration lookup instantly confirms code, variant and compatible replacements
items.cta: Enter your reg to identify your Peugeot 4008 engine code instantly

items.question: What is the most reliable Peugeot 4008 engine?
items.answer: The Mitsubishi-sourced 4B11 2.0L naturally aspirated petrol is mechanically simplest and most robust, but the DV6C diesel offers best balance of economy and reliability when maintained with regular EGR/DPF care.
keyPoints: 4B11 lacks turbo complexity but is rare in UK breaker supply
DV6C diesel achieves 50+ mpg with proactive EGR maintenance
EP6CDB facelift THP offers improved components over early EP6CDT
